text:
Direct Tax & International Taxation  
Portionwise Test 3

Q1

Working Note

Tom Sawyer  FTS  Ganga Ltd  
(NR)  (Ind. Co.)  
(country A)  (agreement approved by CG)  

'No PE in India.'

Answer

a) India has no DTAA with country A
- As per sec 115A when a NR or foreign company receives royalty or fee for technical services from R or Indian co. w.r.t an agreement approved by Central government & such NR does not have PE Permanent establishment in India, then such income shall be taxed @ 20%.
- However, no other deduction or expense benefit is available on the same.
- Since India does not have any DTAA with country A, such income shall be taxed as income & is deemed to be accrue/arise in India u/s 9(1) & taxed as per sec 115A @ 20%.

b) India has DTAA with country A - Taxation rate 5%
- Since in given case DTAA provides tax @ 5%, as per sec 90, provision of Act more beneficial to assessee shall apply.
- As per Income-tax Act, sec 115A tax rate is 20% which is greater than rate offered in DTAA. Hence DTAA shall apply.

I'm unable to extract text from the image directly. However, you can use OCR software or apps to extract handwritten text from images. If you have any questions or need help with something else, feel free to ask!

```plaintext
Q3. Working Note

ABC Ltd
Pepsi Co. Ltd
CoCo Cola Ltd
Machine Cola Co. (UK)
BOAR
Indian Co

- Answer
- A non resident has undertaken or proposes to undertake 
  transaction with resident wherein he wishes to enquire on 
  matter related to question of law or facts, he shall make 
  an application to BOAR.
- BOAR provides ruling against transaction entered or proposed 
  to be undertaken.
- However, if the case in relation to similar transaction is pending 
  with ITA, ITAT, HC or SC then the application shall 
  be rejected by BOAR.
- In given case, Machine Cola Co. has made an application 
  to BOAR relating to rate of withhold tax applicable to it & 
  CoCo Cola Ltd. had made similar application to A.O.
- Since the case is already resting with ITA, the rejection 
  stands justifiable
```

Q4 Working Note

ND Ltd. ← 90 cr loan @10% TM Inc. (London)
(India Co.) ↑ guarantee
40% v.p. ↑
TD Inc. → 40% v.p. → TY Inc. (U.K.)

Proposed Answer

o As per sec 92A, where one enterprise holds more than 26% of voting power directly/indirectly in another enterprise, such enterprise are deemed to be Associate enterprise (A.E.)
o If when 2 enterprises become A.E. of such another enterprise, then such 2 enterprises shall also be A.E.
o Based on above scenario, ND Ltd & TY Inc. are A.E.
o When one A.E. provide guarantee/deposit against loan taken by another A.E. & interest on such loan exceeds 10x then Sec 94B shall apply.
o Max Int allowable on each loan shall be 30% of EBITDA or Actual, whichever is lower. Balance int can be carried forward upto 8 A.Y.
o Calculations
  Particulars ₹ (in cr)
  Net Profit 11
  (+) Dep. 5
  (+) Income Tax 4
  (+) Interest (90 cr x 10%) 9
  EBITDA 29
  30% of EBITDA 8.7
  Actual Interest 9
  c/f Interest 0.3
  Amt of Int. claimed under PGBP 8.7

Sure, here is the extracted handwritten text from the image:

---

QM.

- In case of company, if key managerial decisions or place of effective management is in India, then the company is said to be resident of India.
- In given scenario, ABC Inc. Swedish co. has just setup a liaison office for routine operation support services in India. Significant management & commercial decision are made at Sweden.
- Liaison office is preparatory & auxiliary in nature & does not implies to having a P.E.
- Considering entire above scenario, ABC Inc. is Non resident.

Q8.

Computation of Income under PGBP

Mr. Q A.Y. 25-26

Particulars ₹(in lacs)

Amount received in India:
- Carriage of passenger from Chennai 2
- Carriage of goods from Chennai 1
- Carriage of passenger from Singapore 3

Amount received in Singapore:
- Carriage of passenger from Chennai 1

Total Amount 7

PGBP income (7 x 7.5%) 0.525

PGBP income = 5250000

- In case of presumptive taxation of aircraft, Amount received in India for carriage of goods/passenger outside India & Amount received outside India for carriage of goods or passenger in India shall be treated for computation of PGBP income.

---

Sure, here is the extracted handwritten text:

- PGBP - 7.5% of such income
- No expenditure against the same shall be allowed

In case where Q Airlines (P) Ltd. can thereby claim expenditure of 6.75 cr & pay tax under normal computation.

Q9. OECD action plan 4 basically deals with advance given by R/NR to a Resident enterprise against whose loan guarantee/deposit has been provided by NR who is an associate enterprise of such resident or the loan has been directly given to such resident by its associate non resident enterprise.

- Against such OECD recommended in line to which Sec 94B has been adopted under Income-tax Act 1961.
- Under Sec 94B under such circumstances if value of interest on such advances exceeds 1 cr, then interest allowed as deduction is maximum upto 30% of EBITDA or actual whichever is lower.
- Balance i.e. diff bet'n Actual & allowable shall be carried forward to R A.Y.
